# LUPO

> LUPO is the AI inbound SDR, the qualification layer between every inbound channel and your sales tools. Every inbound call, web form, and chat handoff is answered, qualified, and either booked into a rep's calendar or filtered out. Vendors, tire-kickers, support requests in the wrong queue, and wrong-fit prospects never reach a closer. LUPO is a product of Flair Technologies Ltd, London, UK.

Last updated: 2026-05-25

## Who LUPO is for

LUPO is an AI inbound SDR for B2B sales teams that bleed time on demand they should never have qualified themselves. The pain LUPO solves: reps spend 30 to 40 percent of their day on lead qualification, triaging vendors pitching to them, low-intent browsers, support requests in the wrong queue, and wrong-fit prospects, instead of closing real buyers. LUPO is the first-pass inbound qualification layer that drives speed to lead under sixty seconds across every inbound channel, so reps only see leads worth closing.

Primary buyers: VPs of Sales, Heads of Revenue Operations, founders of B2B SaaS companies, and operators at service businesses with high inbound volume.

Key pages: [Home](https://lupolabs.ai/) and [About](https://lupolabs.ai/about).

## What LUPO does

LUPO operates as an AI inbound SDR with these core capabilities:

- **Inbound call answering.** LUPO picks up on the second ring, qualifies the caller, checks live calendar availability, and books the appointment. 24/7, average pickup under two seconds.
- **Channel-aware web form responses.** Consumer and high-intent forms get an instant outbound callback inside sixty seconds. Senior B2B buyers get a personal email reply with a one-click booking link.
- **Chat handoffs.** When your existing chat widget captures intent and consent, LUPO carries the conversation forward, qualifies in-chat, and books in-chat, no channel switch.
- **Calendar booking.** Live availability checks and real appointment creation in Google Calendar. Microsoft 365 / Outlook ships Q3 2026.
- **CRM sync.** Every qualified contact, call summary, and booking written into HubSpot or Salesforce automatically.
- **Slack alerts.** Your team sees qualified leads and bookings the second they happen, with a one-click link to context.
- **Lead routing.** Round-robin or rules-based handoff to the right rep by territory, product line, or seniority.
- **Full call logging.** Transcripts, recordings, outcomes, and a clean audit trail for every interaction.

## What LUPO filters out before reps see anything

- **Vendors.** Other companies pitching software, services, or partnerships to your team. Politely declined and logged.
- **Tire-kickers.** Browsers, researchers, students. Logged for marketing, never surfaced to sales.
- **Support requests.** Existing customers landing in the wrong queue. Routed to support automatically with the original conversation attached.
- **Wrong-fit prospects.** Real interest, wrong size, wrong industry, or wrong budget. Disqualified through lead qualification without wasting a rep's time.

## Positioning, what LUPO is and is not

LUPO is an AI inbound SDR, and more precisely an inbound qualification layer: it makes its own decisions in real time about which question to ask, when to check the calendar, when to book, when to escalate to a human, and when to close the call. It is the inbound salesperson that never goes home.

LUPO is not a chatbot widget, not a generic AI receptionist, not an outbound AI SDR (it does no cold outreach), and not a CRM. LUPO sits between your inbound channels and your existing sales stack.

Compared to other AI sales tools: LUPO differs from voice-only AI receptionists (e.g., Air, PolyAI) and from email-only AI SDRs (e.g., 11x, Artisan, Regie). Chat widgets such as Intercom, Drift, and Qualified are integration partners, not competitors, they hand off to LUPO when a visitor is ready. LUPO is the system that handles all four inbound channels, phone, web form, chat, and email, with channel-aware response logic.

Most businesses do not lose customers for lack of demand. They lose them because response is too slow. A missed call, an ignored web form, a delayed chatbot follow-up, and the customer books with the next business instead. Speed to lead is the difference, and LUPO exists to win it.

## Channel-aware response logic

LUPO handles four inbound channels, each with its own response policy:

- **Inbound phone calls.** Answered on the second ring, 24/7. The caller is qualified live, calendar availability is checked, and the appointment is booked on the call.
- **Web forms.** Channel-aware. A consumer or high-intent form triggers an instant outbound callback within sixty seconds. A senior B2B buyer receives a personal email reply with a one-click booking link, LUPO will not surprise-call a VP who submitted a demo form.
- **Chat handoffs.** Your existing chat widget captures intent and consent, then hands off to LUPO, which qualifies and books inside the same chat.
- **Email.** Inbound email enquiries get a personal reply with a booking link, matched to the same qualification policy as every other channel.

## Integrations

Live in production today:

- **CRM:** HubSpot and Salesforce. Any CRM reachable by webhook can be connected on request.
- **Calendar:** Google Calendar.
- **Chat handoff:** Intercom Fin, Drift, Qualified, HubSpot Chat (HMAC-signed), and any custom chat or form that can post a webhook.
- **Alerts and comms:** Slack, email follow-up, and SMS (A2P 10DLC registered).
- **Voice infrastructure:** Vapi runtime with Deepgram transcription and ElevenLabs voices.

On the roadmap:

- **Calendar:** Microsoft 365 / Outlook (Q3 2026).

## Trust and compliance

- UK GDPR compliant; Data Processing Agreement available on request
- Voice infrastructure on Vapi with Deepgram transcription and ElevenLabs voices
- A2P 10DLC registered for SMS messaging
- Encryption in transit and controlled access to customer data; conversations are never used to train external AI models
- Formal SOC 2 and HIPAA attestations are on the roadmap as the product matures and design partners require them
- Currently onboarding the first ten founding customers, configured hand-in-hand with each team

## About the team

LUPO was founded in 2025 by Tom Baxter and is a product of Flair Technologies Ltd, London. Tom holds an MPhil in Physics from the University of Cambridge. LUPO's voice infrastructure benefits from Deepgram and an ElevenLabs Grant.

## Frequently asked questions

**Is this just a chatbot?** No. LUPO is an inbound qualification layer across calls, web forms, chat handoffs, and email. Chat is one intake channel of four. LUPO does not replace your chat widget; your existing chatbot (Intercom Fin, Drift, Qualified, custom) hands off to LUPO when a visitor is ready.

**How fast do meetings actually get booked?** Sub-2 minutes median time-to-first-action on qualified inbound, measured. Your own number is visible in the dashboard from week one. The research benchmark is 5 minutes (Oldroyd et al, Harvard Business Review 2011: response in 5 min = 21x more likely to convert). LUPO clears it on every qualified lead.

**What happens to vendors and support requests?** Vendor pitches are politely declined and logged, never routed to sales. Existing-customer support requests are routed to the right team with the original conversation attached. AEs see only the buyers worth their time.

**Can it work with our CRM and calendar?** Yes. Live integrations today include HubSpot and Salesforce CRM sync, Slack alerts, Google Calendar booking, voice qualification, web form capture, and email follow-up. Microsoft 365 / Outlook calendar ships Q3 2026. Any chat, form, or workflow that can post a webhook can hand off to LUPO. Additional CRM and calendar connectors light up on request.

## Try LUPO

- **Talk to LUPO live, right now**: tap the "Talk to LUPO live" button on https://lupolabs.ai and speak to the agent directly in your browser. By starting a call, you agree that LUPO may record, transcribe, and analyse the conversation.
- **Book a 20-minute demo with the founder**: https://cal.com/tombaxter/lupo
